claim: The ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers.

result.claim: The ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers.
result.reasoning: The searches for "ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers," "chromosome ring complexes C. elegans oocytes layered structure," and "C. elegans oocyte chromosome structure" did not yield any direct evidence to support or refute the claim. The retrieved propositions and chunks discuss general aspects of C. elegans development, germline, chromosome modifications, and oocyte maturation, but none specifically mention "ring complexes" or their "discrete layered" construction within C. elegans oocytes. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to verify this claim.

evidence.text: C. elegans development occurs via a series of cell divisions.
evidence.source: Stem cells: the generation and maintenance of cellular diversity

evidence.text: The DRM recruitment motif, DRM binding, and DRM-regulated embryonic genes are under-represented on the X chromosome in C. elegans.
evidence.source: Chromosome-Biased Binding and Gene Regulation by the Caenorhabditis elegans DRM Complex

evidence.text: The nematode roundworm Caenorhabditis elegans connectome was investigated.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: During an asymmetric stem cell division in C. elegans, the new stem cell always assumes the parental cell fate.
evidence.source: Stem cells: the generation and maintenance of cellular diversity

evidence.text: In Caenorhabditis elegans, a cell created by asymmetric division proceeds to form neurons.
evidence.source: C. elegans Runx/CBFβ suppresses POP-1 TCF to convert asymmetric to proliferative division of stem cell-like seam cells.

evidence.text: The nematode roundworm Caenorhabditis elegans has a connectome.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: The Caenorhabditis elegans cell lineage has a precise developmental map.
evidence.source: C. elegans epigenetic regulation in development and aging

evidence.text: In C. elegans, hermaphrodite germ cells first undergo spermatogenesis in larvae followed by a switch to oogenesis.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: Meiosis in oocytes represents a specialized cell division.

evidence.text: C. elegans has seam cell divisions.

evidence.text: In Caenorhabditis elegans, a cell created by asymmetric division differentiates and fuses with the general epidermis.

evidence.text: The key Cdk–cyclin complex that regulates the oocyte cell cycle is known as Cdk1–cyclin B1.
evidence.source: Meiotic Cell Cycle Progression in Mouse Oocytes: Role of Cyclins

evidence.text: Transcriptional inactivity of the X chromosome during male spermatogenesis results in the X chromosome being largely depleted of H3K4me during meiosis and gametogenesis in C. elegans.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: The proliferative cells that support the large numbers of gametes produced by C. elegans adults are GSCs.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: H3K4me2 is stably observed in the chromatin of early dividing blastomeres and germline precursors (P cells) of C. elegans embryos.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: The connectome of C. elegans is almost fully resolved.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: The Set/MLL complex plays important roles in growth and somatic gonad and vulva development in C. elegans.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: Successful contact between mature sperm and oocyte in the C. elegans gonad at the start of fertilization causes the oocyte to release a signal.
evidence.source: Communication between oocytes and somatic cells regulates volatile pheromone production in Caenorhabditis elegans

evidence.text: In Mastl-null oocytes following chromosome segregation in meiosis I, the oocyte chromosomes were rapidly decondensed and the nuclear envelope was reassembled.
evidence.source: Mastl/PP2A regulate Cdk1 in ooycte maturation

evidence.text: The dearth of H3K4me persists in spermatid chromatin in C. elegans.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: WDR-5.1 and RBBP-5 are shared by multiple complexes in C. elegans.
evidence.source: A role for Set1/MLL-related components in epigenetic regulation of the Caenorhabditis elegans germ line.

evidence.text: After cytokinesis, one set of chromosomes is retained in the oocyte and the other is segregated into the first polar body (PB).
evidence.source: Mastl/PP2A regulate Cdk1 in ooycte maturation

evidence.text: H3K4 methylation is densely distributed throughout all chromosomes in the P cell chromatin of C. elegans.
